task:
Atom 1 of the founder-ratified (2026-06-21) ADR-graph reading-contract collapse. This atom writes ONLY the decision/governance layer — no runtime code, no comment retargeting, no ARCHITECTURE tree surgery (those are later atoms). Three edits:

1. PORTABLE RULE -> packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md. FOLD it into the EXISTING 'Elegance Standard' section (do NOT add a new section, do NOT add new vocabulary). The rule, stated as the Elegance Standard's 'one owner per concept' recursed + 'earn it': 'One current-truth surface per architectural SCOPE (not per repo). Within a scope you never chase chains — that surface is the live read; superseded detail is demoted to history that feeds it. A sub-scope earns its OWN surface (with exactly one parent link) when it is independently shipped/owned (its own service/package/deployable) OR its current-truth section outgrows a single screen. Default to one surface; split when earned, never pre-shard. The rule is conditional: it binds only where a repo keeps decisions/architecture worth a current-truth read.' Keep it tight and role-neutral (shared-standards is prepended to every persona; no repo-specific nouns).

2. ADR-0014 (cocoder/decisions/0014-living-adrs.md) -> extend IN PLACE (living-ADR convention; do NOT spawn a new ADR for the rule) to own the meta-statement: 'ADRs feed one current-truth surface per scope and are demoted when superseded.' Add as an amendment consistent with how 0014 already records changes to itself.

3. NEW ADR cocoder/decisions/0031-architecture-reading-contract.md (next free number is 0031) — the CoCoder dogfood instance / reading contract. Status Accepted, dated 2026-06-21, founder-approved. It asserts the reading contract ONLY (no mechanics): ARCHITECTURE.md is the concise current-truth entry point; docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md is the drill-down owner map for orchestration contracts; cocoder/decisions/README.md remains the index/history router; superseded ADRs are demoted to history that feeds ARCHITECTURE.md. Reference that it is the first instance of the ADR-0014 rule. Follow the format/headers used by recent ADRs (e.g. 0030, 0029) and add it to cocoder/decisions/README.md's index. Note the supersession/feeds relationship without rewriting other ADR files in this atom.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) shared-standards rule lives inside the existing Elegance Standard section, no new section, no new noun; (b) ADR-0014 amended in place, no separate ADR for the rule; (c) 0031 exists with proper ADR format + README index entry; (d) `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/personas test` is green (base persona/standards tests still pass); (e) `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` still green if it covers decisions. MUST NOT BREAK: no load-bearing safeguard weakened; no runtime/proof code touched; rule is scope-based not repo-based. WRITE SCOPE: packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md, cocoder/decisions/**, cocoder/decisions/README.md.

Atom 2 of the ADR-graph reading-contract collapse: execute the label/comment/routing mechanics that ADR-0031 deferred. THIS ATOM CHANGES ONLY comments, status banners, and ARCHITECTURE.md prose — NO behavior, NO code logic, NO file moves, NO ADR deletions. It is reversible by construction.

Three groups of edits (the §B ADR-graph verdict enumerated exact sites — confirm each still exists before editing; if a cited line drifted, find the same citation nearby):

(1) DEMOTION BANNERS — only where a live file still reads as current truth:
  - cocoder/decisions/0024-governance-pre-run-snapshot.md: make its status/header say it is PARTIALLY superseded by ADR-0029 (0029 supersedes only step 2 / builder-dirt refusal) while the governance pre-run snapshot / self-heal remainder stays live. Do not mark it fully superseded.
  - cocoder/decisions/0009-extensibility.md and cocoder/decisions/0011-orchestrator-verify-gate.md: ensure each reads clearly as a stable REDIRECT signpost to its merge target (0009→0008, 0011→0013) — tighten only if currently ambiguous.
  - cocoder/decisions/0020-addendum-phase-executor.md: leave Status Superseded (no change needed unless wording is unclear).
  - DO NOT touch cocoder/zArchive/v2/decisions/0015,0021,0022 — they stay frozen history.

(2) RETARGET RUNTIME/PROOF COMMENTS that cite RETIRED ADR numbers as if current owners — comment text only:
  - ADR-0015 lineage comments -> 'ADR-0023 §4 (opt-in isolation/worktree machinery retained)': packages/core/src/commit-gate/git.ts (~:10), packages/core/src/runner/observer.ts (~:23), packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (~:463), and the proof/test lineage comments packages/core/tests/git-worktree.test.ts (~:1) and packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts (~:209) — for the test comments, just label them explicitly historical/lineage.
  - ADR-0011 lineage comments -> 'ADR-0013 verify gate': packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts (~:3), packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts (~:562), packages/core/src/runner/io.ts (~:31).
  - Any ADR-0024 lineage comment that should now point at the founder-vs-agent rule -> 'ADR-0029 (founder pre-run snapshot) lineage'. Find with: rg -n 'ADR-0024|\b0024\b' packages/core/src packages/daemon/src.
  Keep retired numbers ONLY as explicit historical/lineage references (e.g. 'formerly ADR-0011, now ADR-0013'); never as the current owner.

(3) TIGHTEN ARCHITECTURE.md so 'what is true now' reads without chasing chains: spine = ADR-0023 + ADR-0029; orchestration loop = ADR-0013 + ADR-0016 + ADR-0017 + ADR-0026; topology = ADR-0008 + ADR-0012 + ADR-0019 + ADR-0027 + ADR-0030. Keep retired 0015/0021/0022 and the 0020 phase-executor addendum marked as history, not live.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE (run these, paste output):
  - rg -n 'ADR-0015|\bADR-0011\b|0020-addendum|\b0024\b' packages/core/src packages/daemon/src packages/core/tests packages/personas/tests ARCHITECTURE.md  -> every remaining hit is an explicit historical/lineage reference or the correctly-scoped 0024 remainder, NONE present a retired ADR as the current owner.
  - rg -n '^\*\*Status' cocoder/decisions/0024-governance-pre-run-snapshot.md  -> shows partial-supersession-by-0029 wording.
  - node scripts/check-topology.mjs  -> still green.
  - pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -r typecheck  -> green (comment-only edits must not break types).
  - pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/personas test  -> 22/22 green.
MUST NOT BREAK: no behavior change, no logic edits, no ADR/file deletions or moves, no load-bearing safeguard weakened; verify gate / commit spine / write-scope / founder-vs-agent boundary all still owned by their CURRENT ADRs. WRITE SCOPE: ARCHITECTURE.md, cocoder/decisions/**, packages/core/src/**, packages/daemon/src/**, packages/core/tests/**, packages/personas/tests/** (comment/prose/status text only).
